message:
Search: LRU prefix cache, bloom filter, warm worker, manual query builder

Major performance overhaul for search-as-you-type latency targeting
sub-60ms per keystroke. This commit implements multiple optimization
layers that work together:

1. LRU Prefix Cache with Shards
   - CacheShards: HashMap<agent_name, LruCache<query_key, Vec<CachedHit>>>
   - Per-agent shards + "global" shard for multi-agent queries
   - Cache key = query + filters fingerprint (agents/workspaces/time range)
   - Configurable via CASS_CACHE_SHARD_CAP env var (default: 256 entries)
   - On cache hit, filters cached results via hit_matches_query_cached()
   - Tracks metrics: cache_hits, cache_miss, cache_shortfall, reloads

2. Bloom Filter for Fast Rejection
   - Each CachedHit stores bloom64: 64-bit bloom filter of all tokens
   - hash_token() uses djb2-style hash mapped to bit position 0..63
   - bloom_from_text() combines content, title, snippet tokens
   - hit_matches_query_cached() first checks bloom gate before substring
   - Rejects non-matches in <1µs without string operations

3. Background Warm Worker
   - maybe_spawn_warm_worker() creates async task with unbounded channel
   - Receives WarmJob{query, filters} after each search (offset=0 only)
   - Debounces warmups to CASS_WARM_DEBOUNCE_MS (default: 120ms)
   - Runs lightweight 1-doc query to prefill OS cache and Tantivy reader
   - Gracefully shuts down when SearchClient is dropped (Weak reference)

4. Debounced Reader Reload
   - maybe_reload_reader() tracks last_reload timestamp
   - Minimum 300ms between reader.reload() calls
   - Prevents excessive disk I/O during rapid typing
   - Metrics track reload count for debugging

5. Manual Query Construction (replaces QueryParser)
   - Each whitespace-separated token becomes a MUST clause
   - Each token matches via OR of: title, content, title_prefix, content_prefix
   - Uses TermQuery with IndexRecordOption::WithFreqsAndPositions
   - Enables prefix matching via edge n-gram fields from schema v4

6. Improved Snippet Generation
   - is_prefix_only() detects simple alphanumeric queries
   - Skip SnippetGenerator for prefix queries (expensive highlighting)
   - quick_prefix_snippet() and cached_prefix_snippet() for fast fallback
   - Uses preview field when available for instant retrieval

7. Query Sanitization Enhancement
   - sanitize_query() now replaces ALL non-alphanumeric chars with space
   - Matches SimpleTokenizer behavior exactly
   - "c++" → "c", "foo.bar" → "foo bar", "my_variable" → "my variable"

8. Search Priority Change
   - Tantivy is now primary (was SQLite FTS first)
   - Rationale: Tantivy has proper edge n-gram support for prefix matching
   - SQLite FTS5 remains as fallback for consistency edge cases

New tests:
   - cache_prefix_lookup_handles_utf8_boundaries: Unicode safety
   - bloom_gate_rejects_missing_terms: Bloom filter correctness
   - search_matches_prefix_edge_ngram: "cal" matches "calculate"
   - search_matches_snake_case: "vari" matches "my_variable_name"
   - search_matches_symbols_stripped: "c++" and "foo.bar" handling

Part of P12 (Ultra-low-latency search v2) initiative.
Addresses: glt.1 (prefix cache shards), glt.3 (cached-hit fast matcher),
           glt.5 (reader reload debounce)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
